name: Federation Of Greene County Empl.
description_short: Federation of Greene County Employees Federal Credit Union is a small community credit union chartered in 1975, serving 670 members from a single branch in Eutaw, Alabama with $1.34M in assets.
description_long:
Federation of Greene County Employees Federal Credit Union was established on August 25, 1975, under charter number 22131. It operates as a federal credit union with community credit union classification and is headquartered in Eutaw, Alabama. The institution serves a membership base of 670 individuals from a single branch location, maintaining total assets of $1,339,990. Member deposits are protected by NCUA insurance, the standard federal safety net for credit union accounts.

The credit union offers core banking services including check verification, debit and checking account services, loans, and ATM access. They provide credit and lending products alongside deposits and shares accounts. Beyond basic banking, Federation of Greene County Employees FCU offers financial education programs and financial counseling services to their membership, positioning itself as a resource for member financial wellness.

This institution carries a Low Income Designation, indicating its focus on serving underserved populations in Greene County. As a small, community-focused credit union, it prioritizes member relationships over scale. The organization is not a member of the Federal Home Loan Bank system, which limits access to certain wholesale funding mechanisms available to larger institutions.

Federation of Greene County Employees Federal Credit Union is best suited for Greene County employees and members seeking localized, relationship-based banking from a small institution. However, the single-branch model and modest asset size mean limited geographic convenience and potentially narrower product offerings compared to larger credit unions or banks. The institution has no customer ratings yet on the reviewed platform, so reputation assessment is limited. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: NCUA deposit insurance protects member accounts up to standard limits
Low Income Designation indicates commitment to underserved populations
Offers financial counseling services to support member financial wellness
Provides financial education programs alongside basic banking
Community-focused credit union model emphasizes member relationships
Established 49+ years with stable charter history (since 1975)
Check verification services available to members
cons: Only 1 branch location limits physical accessibility for members outside Eutaw
Small asset base ($1.34M) may restrict product variety and lending capacity
No customer ratings or reviews available on platform, making service quality hard to assess
Not a Federal Home Loan Bank member, limiting access to wholesale funding and services
Limited geographic reach restricts membership eligibility to Greene County employees primarily
best_for: Greene County employees seeking community-based banking services
Members prioritizing relationship banking over large-scale institutions
Individuals qualifying for low-income credit union support programs
diagnosis: Federation of Greene County Employees Federal Credit Union is ideal for Greene County employees and residents seeking personalized, community-oriented banking from a small, established credit union with NCUA protection. The primary caveat is the single-branch operation and modest asset base, which may limit convenience and product availability compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
